item.title: The end and the beginning
item.description:
This was my 15 year old puppy Mr Puckett
He and I spent 12 years together, from sky rise condos in Seattle to homeless camps in Minneapolis and everywhere else inbetween
He was there with me through the worst of my addictions, the rock bottoms living on the street, and became a reason for me to fight to get better. After four years of homelessness we finally got back on our feet in 2019 and we moved to Tennessee to settle down.
2 years ago today Mr Puckett passed away. It all happened so fast man, one month he was running 5ks and the next month he was gone. I didn't have a friend within 1,000 miles and I've never felt so lost in my life.
I promised him before he passed that I would never let his name die. The next year I started Puckett Week as a digital charity event in his name. All I knew is that I wanted to help people get off the street like he did with me.
I soon learned that the leading cause of homelessness for women and children in America is domestic violence. I ended up homeless due to my own decisions and consequences. I couldn't fathom women and children having to chose between abuse and going through what I did on the street.
This year in mainly $15 donations, the first two days of Puckett Week brought in over $7,000 for survivors of domestic violence facing homelessness, and over 100 items have been sent to them via an Amazon Wishlist
Most people don't realize that this day is very special to me for another reason. I was so lost after Mr Puckett died, so close to relapse or self harm, I decided to go to an animal shelter just to be near other dogs.
3 hours after he died I took one lap around the shelter and found this beautiful dog. I had never even considered adopting again, but I knew I couldn't go home alone that night.
MrsPuckett saved me that day, much in the same way Mr Puckett did for so many years.
She started running right away, and the more miles we put on the closer we grew. In the 2 years she's been home she's run 2 marathons, over 60 half marathons, and well over 3,000 miles total.
This is my best friend in the whole world.
Now I can look at February 18th as a day of growth rather than a day of remorse. As the incredible life of one dog came to an end, the start of an incredible life for another began.
With a little luck and a lot of work, hopefully these two will never have to know what it was like with Mr Puckett living on the street. With a little help and a lot of love, hopefully we can help abuse survivors get out of the shelter and into a safe home. This is a pretty emotional week for me, and I wanted to thank everyone for being there for me.
The Mary Parrish Center provides free housing for survivors to leave their abusers and stay off the street. In Mr Puckett's honor, I'm asking anyone who can afford it to donate $15 to Mary Parrish and survivors - https://www.maryparrish.org/donate I also had them set up an Amazon Wishlist if you'd like to send items directly to their residents - https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/3USHUHIVSINQE?ref_=wl_share ... Thank you all again for being there for me, and being there for survivors. Mr Puckett may be gone, but I'll never stop trying to do good in his name.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Gamer Tells On Himself After Questioning Why Female Video Game Character Has A ‘Beard’
item.description: A certain gamer dude accidentally told on himself with a Twitter post claiming that the main character from an upcoming sequel to the popular video game Horizon Zero Dawn has a “beard,” apparently unaware that nearly all women have small hairs on their face known often called “peach fuzz.” More in the sauce: https://themotherofallnerds.com/gamer-beard-aloy/
item.dc:creator: moyanerd

item.title: Reposting to amplify. AMERICANS, DO NOT ELECT OR RE-ELECT TRAITORS!

item.title: Day 266: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me

Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.title: Assorted All Video Dump
item.description:
Hey hey Imgurians! I’m in a great mood for no reason whatsoever. A rare high. So I’m gonna ride this out with a wild dump. Check your wifi, buckle up, and stay classy.
Cause why wouldn’t your boat be way up on a cliff.
Fun to send to coworkers to see if their phone volume is on at work lol
I love manufacturing videos of wildly unsafe working conditions.
#5 Many of my Chinese manufacturing videos came from an Imgurian that hasn’t posted in a while.
I haven’t been on Imgur long, but I used to search for his username before I even had an account so I could see what new gems he posted.
When I joined, I messaged him. Like, one of the first things I did. He hadn’t been active for a few months at that point. I fear Covid or some other catastrophe got him. Sigh.
#10
#15
#20
#25
#30
Cat tax
#35
#40
#45
Joey tax Hope you all enjoyed the dump. Have a fantastic rest of your day and have a safe memorable weekend!
item.dc:creator: knowallknownothing

item.title: Full Scale Homemade Arcade "Tile World" I Made From Scratch
item.description:
I started building mechanical arcade games from scratch a few years ago. When stuck inside 2020 I started to design and build a completely original arcade with my friend Sam. It is called Tile World.
At Tile World you are given a small "Play Tile". This keeps your balance of Tile Tokens and Tile Tickets. Tile Tokens are the currency you need to play games. Tile Tickets earn you prizes.
We have 11 original machines in total and this was one of the first games we made. You have to count the number of tulips you see in each harvest and level up if you guess correctly (or close to correct)
This is a more physical games. You throw the balls into the holes to "plant" tulip bulbs. At Tile World we have no screens. All scores and info are displayed on moving tiles and with the assistance of bells (instead of screens).
This is one of my favorite machines. Instead of a game it is a jukebox where our mascot (Tilly the Tulip) conducts the songs.
At the end of playing you can (if you've won enough tile tickets) take home a prize. We have a vending machine for tiles. We also have a tile gumball machine.
This is another one of our favorite games 'Willy's Wickets'. You try to time the release of a marble down three winding paths. Score is kept through marbles up top.
There's too much to show in one post! But if you are interested in learning more or visiting one day you can message me, follow us at https://www.instagram.com/builtbyblatt/, or visit http://tileworldarcade.com
item.dc:creator: BuiltByBlatt
